app.name: Kokoro Engine
app.one_liner: Open-source immersive character engine for desktop AI companions.

screens.name: Main Chat Interface
screens.purpose: Primary interaction surface for conversing with the AI companion, displaying chat history, Live2D avatar, and pending action approvals.

screens.name: Settings Panel
screens.purpose: Configure engine behavior, model preferences, MODs, and system options with tab persistence across sessions.

rust_commands.name: cancel_turn
rust_commands.purpose: Cancel the current AI processing turn at defined pipeline checkpoints.

rust_commands.name: execute_tool_action
rust_commands.purpose: Execute a staged tool action with canonical identity, audit logging, and user approval trace.

rust_commands.name: load_mod_manifest
rust_commands.purpose: Load and validate MOD manifest with declarative capabilities declaration.

mvp_plan: week 1: Build basic MOD loading with manifest validation
week 1: Implement core chat pipeline with turn-level cancellation
week 1: Integrate Live2D cue system with unified profiles
week 1: Set up SQLite storage for local-first data persistence
week 2: Add centralized Hook Runtime for chat/tools/MODs
week 2: Add multilingual support for core UI strings
week 2: Enable TTS lifecycle hooks and Edge TTS integration
week 2: Implement staged tool action policy with approval UI
acceptance_tests: Conversation summaries are generated and stored locally after each session.
MODs declare capabilities in manifest; engine enforces permissions at runtime.
Settings tab selection persists across application restarts.
Tool execution requires explicit user approval; denied tools are logged with canonical identity and do not run.
User can cancel an ongoing AI turn before completion, and the system halts all associated actions (TTS, tool calls).
